title: Aging and Geriatric Health

part.part_title: Special Populations and Life Course Approach

learning_objectives.text: Define key concepts of population aging, healthy aging, and active aging
List major age-related diseases and geriatric syndromes
Identify components of WHO's framework for healthy aging
Explain the global demographic transition and its health implications
Describe the relationship between intrinsic capacity and functional ability
Summarize different models of long-term care provision
Apply age-friendly principles to community health program design
Use comprehensive geriatric assessment tools in care planning
Implement healthy aging interventions in diverse cultural contexts
Analyze population aging trends and their health system implications
Compare effectiveness of different long-term care models
Examine social determinants influencing healthy aging outcomes
Evaluate the sustainability of long-term care financing mechanisms
Assess the impact of age-friendly environment interventions
Critique current approaches to addressing ageism in healthcare
Design comprehensive healthy aging programs for different economic contexts
Develop age-friendly community initiatives using evidence-based approaches
Create integrated care models for multimorbid older adults

case_studies.title: Japan's Long-Term Care Insurance System
case_studies.focus: Universal long-term care coverage and healthy aging promotion
key_lessons: Design of universal long-term care insurance systems
Integration of health promotion and care services
outcomes: Improved access to long-term care services
Enhanced quality of life for older adults
challenges: Rising care costs with population aging

case_studies.title: WHO Global Network of Age-Friendly Cities and Communities
key_lessons: Adaptation to local contexts and resources
outcomes: Enhanced social participation of older adults

case_studies.title: Brazil's Family Health Strategy for Older Adults
case_studies.focus: Primary healthcare integration for geriatric care
key_lessons: Integration of geriatric care in primary health systems
Community health worker training for elderly care

case_studies.title: South Africa's Social Pension System and Healthy Aging
case_studies.focus: Social protection and poverty reduction for older adults
key_lessons: Impact of social grants on health outcomes
outcomes: Improved nutritional status of older adults

sdg_alignment.description: No Poverty (social protection for older adults)
Good Health and Well-being (healthy aging promotion)
Gender Equality (addressing gendered aspects of aging)
Peace and Justice (rights-based approach to aging)

practical_exercises.title: Comprehensive Geriatric Assessment Design
practical_exercises.objective: Design a comprehensive geriatric assessment protocol for use in primary healthcare settings
instructions: Select a specific country context (provided case scenarios)
Identify relevant assessment domains and tools
Consider cultural adaptations and resource constraints
Develop implementation strategy and training plan
Create quality assurance and monitoring framework
practical_exercises.deliverable: Complete assessment protocol with implementation guide

practical_exercises.title: Age-Friendly Community Initiative Planning
practical_exercises.objective: Develop an age-friendly community initiative for a specified setting
instructions: Engage stakeholders and older adults in planning
Design interventions across WHO age-friendly domains
Create implementation timeline and resource plan
practical_exercises.deliverable: Comprehensive age-friendly community plan with budget

practical_exercises.title: Long-Term Care Policy Analysis
practical_exercises.objective: Analyze and compare long-term care policies across different countries
instructions: Select three countries with different income levels
Map long-term care systems and financing mechanisms
Analyze coverage, accessibility, and quality indicators
Recommend policy improvements based on evidence

items.question: According to WHO's framework for healthy aging, which component is most fundamental?
items.explanation: WHO's framework emphasizes functional ability as the health-related attribute that enables well-being, comprising intrinsic capacity and environmental factors.

items.question: The demographic dividend refers to:
options: A) Economic growth from increased healthcare spending
B) Benefits from declining dependency ratios
C) Increased productivity from population aging
D) Healthcare savings from preventive interventions
items.explanation: The demographic dividend occurs when declining birth and death rates result in a lower dependency ratio, providing economic opportunities.

items.question: Which intervention has shown the strongest evidence for preventing falls in community-dwelling older adults?
items.explanation: Multifactorial interventions addressing multiple risk factors have demonstrated the strongest evidence for fall prevention in community settings.

items.question: Japan's Long-Term Care Insurance system is primarily financed through:
options: C) Combination of social insurance and taxation
items.explanation: Japan's system combines social insurance premiums from those aged 40+ with government funding from national and local taxes.

items.question: The concept of 'aging in place' primarily emphasizes:
options: A) Remaining in the family home at all costs
C) Staying in familiar environments with appropriate support
D) Avoiding institutional care regardless of needs
items.explanation: Aging in place focuses on enabling older adults to remain in familiar environments with necessary support services to maintain independence and quality of life.

items: Explain the difference between intrinsic capacity and functional ability in WHO's healthy aging framework.
Describe three key components of an age-friendly community and provide examples of specific interventions.
Compare and contrast family-based care and institutional care models for older adults, including advantages and challenges.
Analyze the relationship between population aging and health system sustainability in low and middle-income countries.
Discuss the role of technology in supporting healthy aging and identify potential barriers to implementation.

summative_assessments: Multiple choice questions on aging concepts
Short answer questions on care models
Essay on long-term care policy analysis

required_readings: WHO Global Strategy and Action Plan on Ageing and Health 2016-2020
Beard et al. The World report on ageing and health: a policy framework for healthy ageing. Lancet. 2016;387(10033):2145-2154.
Chang et al. Measuring population ageing: an analysis of the Global Burden of Disease Study 2017. Lancet Public Health. 2019;4(3):e159-e167.
Rudnicka et al. The World Health Organization Quality of Life-BREF questionnaire: psychometric properties and normative data for older adults. Qual Life Res. 2006;15(5):853-865.
supplementary_resources: WHO Global Network for Age-friendly Cities and Communities portal
HelpAge International country profiles and reports
OECD Health at a Glance aging indicators
